exemplar.title: Writing a guidebook for middle and high school students

detailedExemplarData.title: Writing a guidebook for middle and high school students

strengths: Effective documentation of time-management and critical-thinking ATL skills using a digital planner and structured sessions.
Described creativity skills application with humor, mind maps, and ChatGPT brainstorming linked to reader engagement.
Clear framing of ATL usage that naturally transitions into detailed evidence showing skill application toward the learning goal.
weaknesses: Needs to explain how each ATL skill directly impacted product quality with evidence such as improved readability or feedback scores.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: In applying skills, you have effectively recorded how time-management, critical-thinking, and creative ATL skills supported your process, showcasing concrete strategies and tactics. This clear framing underpins your learning journey. To strengthen this section, **connect each skill to tangible improvements in your final product**âfor example, cite reader feedback metrics or readability scoresâto demonstrate the real impact of these skills on the quality of your guide.

strengths: Detailed chapter sequencing and content scope in the âStudy Like a Proâ outline demonstrates strong alignment with the product goal.
Clear weekly and monthly plan sequencing tasks supports consistent progress and aligns with product development milestones.
Structured narrative under âPlan for achieving my productâ effectively breaks down the process into actionable steps.
weaknesses: Learning goal lacks a concise, measurable statement and immediate clarity.
Product goal and success criteria lack quantified targets and specific evaluation metrics.
Timeline and resource details (software, interview contacts, deadlines) are not fully specified, and later project phases are unplanned.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning section shows **strong structural organization** through detailed sequencing and clear task scheduling; you have laid out a coherent roadmap for developing your guide. However, the **learning goal and success criteria** need greater specificity and measurabilityâdefining precise targets and testing methods will sharpen your focus. Additionally, fleshing out the timeline with all project phases and specifying required resources and tools (e.g., software, interview contacts, deadlines) will ensure your plan is fully feasible and actionable.

strengths: Provided clear evidence of research depth through online courses, interviews, and literature supporting the learning goal.
Described growth in communication skills via initiating interviews and surveys, demonstrating increased self-confidence.
Demonstrated reflection on development of writing, design, organization, and time-management skills, linking these to product effectiveness.
weaknesses: Reflection does not explicitly evaluate the final guide against each success criterion; missing a mapped outcomes section.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflections richly capture your learning journey, highlighting the depth of your research, the evolution of your communication skills, and improvements in writing, design, organization, and time-management. These examples illustrate genuine personal growth. To complete this section, **systematically evaluate your final product against each original success criterion**, mapping specific outcomes and evidence to demonstrate how well you achieved your stated goals and where further refinements might be made.

annotations.comment: Your use of time-management and critical-thinking ATL skills is well-documented with concrete strategies (digital planner, structured sessions). This shows clear effectiveness in organizing your research.
The product goal outlines content well but lacks detail on timeline, required resources and tools. Specify which software, interview contacts, and dates you will use to ensure feasibility.
Your detailed outline for âStudy Like a Proâ demonstrates comprehensive sequencing of chapters and content scope, reflecting strong planning and alignment with your product goal.
Your âLearning Goalâ header sets the section but a concise statement following it would help readers immediately grasp your specific objective.

Your learning goal narrative is engaging but lacks a clear, measurable statement. Define exactly what you will achieve and how you will measure success (e.g., âincrease my ability to explain study methods using flashcards in peer sessions by 80%â).

You describe your interest in writing and helping others, but the link between that interest and your learning goal needs specific evidence. Explain how personal experiences will inform your guideâs content with concrete examples.
You provide clear evidence of research depthâonline courses, interviews, and literatureâwhich effectively supports your learning goal achievement.
Your description of communication skill growthâinitiating interviews and surveysâdemonstrates self-confidence and effective collaboration, enriching your guideâs credibility.
Your success-criteria table lists relevant attributes but testing methods are vague. For each criterion, add specific evaluation metrics (e.g., â90% positive feedback from 10 teen readersâ).
Your weekly and monthly plan clearly sequences tasks, balancing research, writing, and revisions. This structure supports consistent progress and aligns well with product development milestones.
To strengthen your ATL-to-product link, explain how each creative element directly improved readability or feedback scores, evidencing impact on quality rather than listing features.
Your reflection on writing, design and organization skills shows specific examples of growth and links these to improved product effectiveness.
You articulate how your time-management improved through weekly/monthly planningâthis concrete evidence illustrates significant personal development.
The task table shows completed steps but leaves later months unplanned. Extend this schedule through all project phases, specifying deadlines for feedback collection and final proofing.
The âSuccess criteriaâ header frames this section well; ensure each criterion below is directly linked to measurable testing methods for clarity.
The creativity skills section describes specific tactics (humor, mind maps, ChatGPT brainstorming) and links them to reader engagement, demonstrating impactful application for product quality.
Your reflection opening summarizes growth but does not explicitly evaluate the final guide against each success criterion. Add a section that maps outcomes back to your initial criteria.
The âProduct goalâ header introduces the section effectively, but the goal itself needs quantified targets (e.g., âfinalize 30 pages by March 1stâ).
Your âPlan for achieving my productâ header signals a structured approach; the narrative that follows effectively breaks down your process into actionable steps.
Under âApplying skillsâ, your framing of ATL usage is clear and leads naturally into detailed evidence, supporting how these skills met your learning goal.
